review_text:
Was exactly what I needed! Was the most beautiful stay and accommodation, when we arrived the place was spotless and I must say the park manager was absolutely amazing. She offered us help to do a fire pit and we came back from doing our groceries and she had already set it up for us, such a kind gesture and her hospitality and help was outstanding. The tiny away team have been so professional and fast responding with any concerns/changes needed.

100% recommend, will be going back!
(Granite belt, Tiny away)

- Angelina g
We loved our trip at tiny away! It was the perfect reminder to slow down and reset! The cabins were so well done and kept it really did just feel like a home away from home. I’ll definitely be back!

Great place
Friendly staff
great amenities

We had a lovely stay. It was a great location, tucked away in nature. Pippa was very warm and welcoming, and provided helpful information. All amenities were available. I highly recommend for a getaway trip.
Absolutely loved the stay here. Such beautiful accommodation and Pippa our host was lovely!

Wow what a fantastic property on storm king dam, with new modern tiny homes so you have all the comforts while enjoying an evening fire and great fishing. The lady at reception was lovely and so helpful. 100% recommend tiny escape. Ill be back :)

We relished our stay in our peaceful lakeside cabin. The cabin was spacious and well appointed. The cabins are located separately from the tourist park, and are quiet and serene. We enjoyed listening to birdsong as we enjoyed afternoons on the deck. Evening fires in the fireplace kept us cosy. Morning walks were a highlight. Would happily visit again.

We always love staying at your complex and have been for many years now. We love the new tiny homes. The beds are very comfortable and the facilities are terrific. Perhaps to make the tiny homes a little more comfortable would be a screen door so you can allow fresh air in without flies and bugs flying in and also an awning would help with cooling it down and making the deck more useable when sunny.
We booked it last minute and it was a good experience.  The toilet is a bit of a letdown but the hot shower makes up for it. All in all, still worth a try.

The park is excellence plenty of space to move about the dam to have a swim in far away from Stanthorpe to town to just be out on your own thoroughly recommended the little houses very comfortable and the chalets fully appointed sit on the deck and watch the sun go down while enjoying a barbecue with friends

Omg I will always come back to this place the staff are wonderful and the are is great they have a dam right near then with all amenities kids area games room BBQs and also have family untils available and they have fire pit and a pool for the family area 5 stars for me loved it

We took 14 members of our rowing club to Sommerville Valley Tourist Park (old name) and it was perfect. Booked 2 x 3 bedroom cabins and had a fantastic time.
Some members had camping trailers/caravans and so they were able to stay very close also.
Rooms were clean and spacious, views amazing and facilities great. Pippa was brilliant to deal with.
Have already booked for next year!!
Absolutely magic spot located short drive from town. Kids playground as well.
Great spot next to lake. Tiny homes are really comfy and everything you need. Easy check-in and out. Perfect winery and walking weekend !
Been coming back for years, a great place to stay.

Lovely place to visit. The camp kitchen is one of the best I've seen with access to fridges, gas stoves etc.  The only reason I didn't give a 5 star review was due to the cost to stay there and the showers only have a 5 min timer which is impossible if you need to wash long hair or children.

What a place to relax and unwind, the views over storm king from the deck of the cabin are amazing.
The moment we arrived Pippa the host was extremely welcoming and went above and beyond for our stay, if I could give 6 stars I would, will definitely be coming back to this hidden gem.
Caravan park very clean and nice area.

Absolutely Loved It!
We stayed in one of the mobile homes and it had everything we needed – clean, modern, and super comfortable. The whole place was tidy and well looked after. Communication was great from the beginning, no problems with the late check in and the team made everything easy. Everything smelled fresh and new. Definitely exceeded our expectations – we’ll be coming back for sure!

Lots of open spaces and plenty of room to walk your furry friend.. on leash, or they’ll be off chasing rabbits.

Peaceful with great amenities.

**Absolutely Love This Place – 10 Years and Still Coming Back! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐**

We've been visiting Tiny Away Escape for over a decade—our first stay in a powered tent area was when our son was just 4 years old, and now he's 14, and upgraded from small cabin to the apartment.  This place has truly become our family’s favourite getaway.

Every single time, the cleanliness is impeccable. The cabins are always spotless, fresh, and well looked after. It’s clear that a lot of care goes into every detail.

The location is so peaceful and relaxing—surrounded by nature and completely away from the hustle and bustle. It’s the perfect spot to unwind and recharge.

The staff, Peepa, Jill and the caretakers are amazing—so friendly, welcoming, and genuinely care about your experience. Over the years, they’ve become like familiar faces we look forward to seeing again.

We’ve never stopped coming back, and we don’t plan to anytime soon. Highly recommend this hidden gem to anyone looking for spotless accommodation, tranquility, and warm hospitality.

Our family of four had a lovely stay at the cabin, which came with a cozy bunk bed setup. The site is beautifully located—not too far from town and within easy reach of Girraween National Park. Shared facilities are excellent overall, though the 5-minute timer on the hot water system can be a bit limiting when multiple showers are in use at once.

Campfires require an off-ground fire pit, so if you don’t have one, make sure to borrow one from the office. Pippa was incredibly kind and helpful, patiently answering all our questions.

5 Stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
A Wonderful Winter Escape!

We just spent 5 fantastic days at Sommerville Valley Tourist Park during the Stanthorpe Snowflake Festival, and it was the perfect base for our family getaway. My wife and I stayed in a non-ensuite cabin with our two young boys, and we were thoroughly impressed. The accommodation was clean, comfortable, and perfectly suited to our needs.

The communal bathrooms were exceptionally clean and well-maintained — a huge plus when travelling with kids! The park itself is beautifully spacious, giving our boys plenty of room to run around and burn off some energy in between festival activities.

A special shoutout to Phillipa from the staff — she went above and beyond to ensure our stay was smooth and enjoyable. Her warm, helpful attitude really made us feel welcome.

We're already planning a return visit and would love to bring our elderly parents along next time. The 3-bedroom resort cabin on the water looks ideal for a relaxing, multi-generational family trip.

Highly recommend Sommerville Valley Tourist Park to anyone looking for a peaceful, family-friendly getaway in the Granite Belt region.

Absolutely beautiful. We stayed in the 3bedroom cabin. We arrived and the fire place was full of kindling and ready to be lit with fire lighters and matches ready. Wood stacked up outside to use on the inside fire. The fire warmed the house so well and such a beautiful touch was the hot water bottles on the beds! The spa bath was amazing. Very clean and very comfortable. The view over the lake is stunning and we had our own Jetty also which was so useful for our hens weekend photos.  The outside fire is also fantastic but you do need to bring your own wood for that. Wine tours pick you up right from the door so that’s a bonus. Highly recommend staying here!!!!
Great location to relax and unwind, loads of wildlife, lakeside views… Combined with great hosts and facilities, an absolute must for us …

Beautiful place to stay
Very affordable and clean

Very nice place to stay we were in cabins the 3 bedroom ones. Nice and tidy would recommend great view of the dam. Reception a little limited near the cabins not sure near the camp kitchen as they have WiFi there.

We were overcharged and when I explained that a family member had surgery and couldn't attend, they refused to listen and still charged them I sent all the hospital infos. The team was unhelpful and unwilling to assist us. While I paid the remaining balance, I received countless emails and texts about it. Be cautious with this company. They claimed to have facilities, but we couldn’t find them in the houses.
Stayed in the resort cabins,  great spot on the dam. Very pretty. Large cabins and lovely hosts. Would stay again!
Great camping with good facilities. Very well kept camp grounds.

Top spot, not too far from town but a perfect secluded spot. Plenty of space between vans and the dam view is a perfect way to start the morning with your favourite cuppa. And you have the option of having your very own camp fire. Be assured to book this one when looking for that perfect wind down.
We had a powered site just up from the dam and it was gorgeous!! Such a beautiful relaxing peaceful place and we'll definitely be back.

This is the most comfortable campsite I’ve stayed at so far. Friendly staff and very clean, well-maintained facilities. I’m really thankful to the staff for keeping the toilets and kitchen so clean. Beautiful environment — definitely recommend!

Booked a site for three days and stayed five. Really large green grassy sites. Great starting point to see all Granit Belt has to offer. Excellent for dogs and/or kids if you have them. Facilities are excellent - as are staff. Highly recommend and would return if in the district.
Awesome experience and cabins were so clean and setup properly. The host was very friendly.
Relaxing.

Loved my stay here! The area is fantastic, it was my first time in Stanthorpe and there are so many things to do. Stunning sunsets by the water, the whole staff is so nice and Pippa is really an expert at giving the best recommendations

Amazing location, especially if you want to explore Girraween and Stanthorpe.

Cabin 7 has a powerful sensor activated flood light positioned above the door, which attracted many insects that made their way into the cabin when entering or existing. This caused us a lot of grief.

No bin or bin bags seemed like a huge oversight, especially when travelling with a family.

No screen door or fan to control indoor temperature.

While we understood this was a low cost cabin, these few things mounted to be considerable draw backs when travelling with two adults and two children.

Shower/toilet block was adequate.

Staff where lovely.

Pippa is so lovely to deal with. Her customer service is excellent. Storm King  dam is there for swimming and kayaking. You can fish too. Each campsite has a firepit. You can purchase firewood, ice, etc, from the office. The amenities are kept clean and you can have a great quick shower.

We trek out here every winter for a week - it is unbeatable in terms of beauty, location, and service. The park and amenities are always clean, sites are large and positioned to ensure privacy, and Pippa in the office is simply gorgeous. I note people's reviews complaining about patchy phone reception, but for us that's part of the attraction; why go looking for peace and solitude only to complain that your internet isn't spot on? 😅 This is one of our favourite places anywhere... absolutely divine.

I love caravanning here. First June trip and the cool breeze definitely makes it crisp!! Definitely extra blankets.  Amenities are spotless, camp kitchen clean and indoors with a rec room. Pippa has been amazing helping my small group have the best time.

Absolutely majestic place in Stanthorpe. We pitched a tent for the night and had a nice hot shower with all the amenities & gtounds clean & well presented for 2 old bikers. The sunrise was amazing. Thanks Sommerville Valley Tourist Park

We drove from Gold Coast to Stanthorpe for a 3 night stay at Somerville Tourist Park arriving on the Saturday morning 10 April 2024. Upon arrival we were allocated a small cabin which was relatively comfortable and clean. Opposite us was the SKIP Bin by 25 metres which was directly outside the Toilet/Shower block. That SKIP Bin was completely full of rubbish and when I initially opened it i was confronted by a 'swarm' of flies which flew into my face. I slammed the lid and refrained from using that bin again. For the next 72 hours the SKIP Bin was never emptied or moved and remained completely full and was absolutely disgusting and no attempt to empty it was made and the flies continued to enter our Unit. We departed the Park on Monday about 9.00 am for the day and the SKIP Bin still hadn't been emptied. I attended the Office on the Saturday and Sunday to ask that the SKIP Bin be emptied however no one was in attendance.
I went to the office again about 8.00 am on the Monday and no one was in  attendance. We then left the Park for the day and never saw anyone in the office after that. We departed the park about 9.00 am on the Tuesday and the SKIP Bin still hadn't been emptied. Quite unacceptable and a very negative experience.
Never again!
fantastic camp grounds, huge van spaces ,spotless facilities ,thank you PIPPA will be back soon.

We stayed mid January at the resort part of the park, the cabins are spacious and comfortable, we had a 3 bedroom one bathroom cabin, they were clean enough, a few negatives were: the driveway to get to the cabins is extremely rough with deep potholes, the cabin has one ceiling fan which didn’t work and no air conditioning  it was extremely hot and humid weather so this made our stay practically unbearable, we had to purchase fans as there was no air flow through the cabin, we had booked for 10 days to visit family in the Stanthorpe area and Warwick and checked out 2 days early as the heat and no air con was very unpleasant, the pool was dirty and hadn’t been cleaned the entire time we were there, the kitchen is stocked with JUST the basics, the amount of money we paid I was expecting ALOT more than what is offered, air con split system would be a good start to improve these cabins as I would imagine even in winter the small wood fire heater inside would not be enough to keep it warm, the scenery is very beautiful and it’s a private area to stay which was nice, it served its purpose for us, we got to spend time with our extended family but would not stay here again, the cabins need a freshen up to justify the amount they charging
Great camping spot. Views spot on and the services on Point, have come here every year for 6 years now.
Terrible Terrible phone coverage. Optus is non existent on-site.  The Wifi they offer is awful and basically does not work at all.

We phoned...No answer.
We drove out all the way there... No one answered.
There were empty cabins available, but we couldn't use them.
We drove all the way back to Stanthorpe and found a much better accommodation at The Vines Motel.

Really happy with our time here.
Staff was friendly and facilities clean.
Grassy spacious sites. Nice and quiet. Lots of Hares which kept the kids entertained. Amenities clean. Kids facilities basic. Good value, would stay again.

Great spot. Just a few kilometres from Stanthorpe but a very peaceful and secluded spot. Great staff and excellent amenities. Great sized sites. Don't miss this one. Right on the dam and great bushland surroundings.
Our group had a great weekend.  Facilities were well kept and other campers were very friendly.
Very clean toilets and showers.  6 minutes' worth of hot water was plenty.  Clean camp kitchen.

Lovely quiet park. Good spot for visiting Girraween National park. Lovely view of the storm dam

Power kept tripping our aircon in our motor home. May want to get that fixed. It hasn’t happened before or after we went there

Has all the facilities you would need. They leave the information you need on the door to the office, if you arrive after hours. There is a fire pit in the middle of the un-powered sites. Just bring firewood or you can buy it on site as well. The network is pretty spotty but I got better reception near the office(Optus).

Great caravan park, large drive through sites, the grounds very well kept.|Pippa in the office is amazing, customer service is impeccable.|Very friendly atmosphere to the whole place.|The amenities blocks are spotless, timed showers but plenty of time to have a good shower, with hot water. |The views are amazing, great views of Storm King Dam.|We have been going here for many yards and will continue for many years more, |You can have a camp fire if you  bring your own fire pit, fire wood available on site.|We highly recommend this park,
